text:
(a) The pilot board of a navigation district shall examine and determine the qualifications of each applicant for the position of branch or deputy pilot before appointing a branch or deputy pilot.

(b) The pilot board shall specify a period that an applicant must reside in this state immediately before the person’s appointment. The period may not exceed two years.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 4
### Chapter 62. Navigation Dist. Pilot Boards
##### § 62.041 — Branch or Deputy Pilot Qualifications

(a) The pilot board of a navigation district shall examine and determine the qualifications of each applicant for the position of branch or deputy pilot before appointing a branch or deputy pilot.

(b) The pilot board shall specify a period that an applicant must reside in this state immediately before the person’s appointment. The period may not exceed two years.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The pilot board of a navigation district shall examine and determine the qualifications of each applicant for the position of branch or deputy pilot before appointing a branch or deputy pilot.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The pilot board shall specify a period that an applicant must reside in this state immediately before the person’s appointment. The period may not exceed two years.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
